content:
[S1]: Antibiotic resistance is when some tiny germs, called bacteria, stop being killed by the medicines doctors use to fight infections.
[READER]: Hmm, I didn’t know germs could become strong like that! I want to know more about how that happens.

[S2]: This happens because bacteria can change and learn to protect themselves from antibiotics.
[READER]: Wow, bacteria can learn? That sounds like they’re getting smarter. How do they change?

[S3]: When people use antibiotics too much or in the wrong way, some bacteria survive and pass on their strong traits to new bacteria.
[READER]: Oh, so taking medicine the wrong way makes the germs stronger? That sounds important to understand.

[S4]: Sometimes, people stop taking antibiotics too soon, so not all the bacteria are killed.
[READER]: So if I feel better and stop medicine, the germs might still be there? That’s a little scary!

[S5]: These surviving bacteria then multiply and can make other people sick with infections that are hard to treat.
[READER]: Yikes! That means more people can get sick and it’s harder to fix them.

[S6]: Also, doctors sometimes give antibiotics when they aren’t really needed, like for colds caused by viruses.
[READER]: Wait, colds aren’t caused by bacteria? Then why would antibiotics be given? That sounds confusing.

[S7]: That’s right! Antibiotics don’t work on viruses, so using them then just helps bacteria become stronger for no reason.
[READER]: Oh, now I get it! Using antibiotics only when they’re really needed makes sense.

[S8]: To help stop antibiotic resistance, it’s important to take medicines exactly as the doctor says and not share them with others.
[READER]: Okay, I will remember that! Taking medicine properly is really important.

[S9]: Scientists are also working hard to find new medicines and ways to fight these strong bacteria.
[READER]: Good! It’s nice to know people are trying to help us stay safe from germs.

[S10]: So, by using antibiotics carefully and listening to doctors, we can keep these medicines working well for everyone.
[READER]: I want to tell my family about this so we can all help stop the germs from getting stronger!

===BEGIN===
Antibiotic resistance is when some tiny germs, called bacteria, stop being killed by the medicines doctors use to fight infections. This happens because bacteria can change and learn to protect themselves from antibiotics. When people use antibiotics too much or in the wrong way, some bacteria survive and pass on their strong traits to new bacteria. Sometimes, people stop taking antibiotics too soon, so not all the bacteria are killed. These surviving bacteria then multiply and can make other people sick with infections that are hard to treat. Also, doctors sometimes give antibiotics when they aren’t really needed, like for colds caused by viruses. That’s right! Antibiotics don’t work on viruses, so using them then just helps bacteria become stronger for no reason. To help stop antibiotic resistance, it’s important to take medicines exactly as the doctor says and not share them with others. Scientists are also working hard to find new medicines and ways to fight these strong bacteria. So, by using antibiotics carefully and listening to doctors, we can keep these medicines working well for everyone.
===END===
